key_points: Human beings adapt to different environments like mountains, deserts, plains, and coastal areas
Activities like agriculture, settlement, and trade depend on the natural environment
Natural hazards such as floods, cyclones, droughts, and landslides affect human life
Humans modify the environment through deforestation, irrigation, and urbanization
Sustainable practices help balance human needs and environmental protection
examples: Terrace farming in hilly areas prevents soil erosion
Desert communities store water and build mud-brick houses to survive
River plains support fertile agriculture but are prone to floods
Cyclone shelters and embankments protect coastal villages
Wetlands act as natural flood buffers and water purifiers
formulas: Adaptation → Adjusting lifestyle to suit environment
Modification → Changing environment for human use
Disaster → Natural hazard affecting human life
Sustainable Use → Meeting present needs without harming future resources
exam_tips: Remember examples of human adaptation in different environments
Focus on how humans modify the environment and its consequences
Learn major natural hazards and measures to reduce their impact
Understand the importance of sustainable practices in daily life
